meta.title: Circuit Loading Rules — NAC and SLC Current Limits
meta.description: Maximum device loading per circuit type with current draw references

notification_appliance_circuits_nac.description: NACs (Notification Appliance Circuits) power the audible and visual notification devices

max_current_per_nac.typical_range: 0.5A to 3.0A per circuit (manufacturer and panel specific)

current_draw_reference.description: Typical current draw at 24VDC for common notification appliances

loading_calculation.formula: Total NAC current = Σ (current_draw of each device on the circuit)
loading_calculation.voltage_drop_check: Must verify voltage at farthest device ≥ 20VDC (typically 16V minimum operating)
loading_calculation.rule: Total current must NOT exceed panel NAC rating

example.voltage_drop: Must calculate based on wire length — see voltage-drop.json

end_of_line_resistor_nac.notes: Always use EXACT value specified by panel manufacturer

initiating_device_circuits_idc.description: IDCs (Initiating Device Circuits) carry signals from detectors and pull stations to the panel

signaling_line_circuits_slc.description: SLCs (Signaling Line Circuits) are the digital communication loops in addressable systems

max_devices_per_slc.notes: Check manufacturer specification — exceeding limits causes communication failures

rules: Each device must have a unique address on its SLC loop
Address must be programmed into the device (DIP switches or electronic)
Duplicate addresses on the same loop cause communication failure
Some panels allow auto-learn of device addresses during commissioning

circuit_class_summary.description: Wire supervision class affects reliability and redundancy

classes.description: Single path — EOL device at end
classes.fault_response: Trouble signal on open or short
classes.operation_on_fault: Devices on fault side of break go offline

classes.description: Redundant path — return wire from last device back to panel
classes.fault_response: Trouble signal on open, but circuit still operates
classes.operation_on_fault: All devices remain operational on single fault
classes.use_when: Life safety critical, hospitals, high-rise, code-required

classes.description: Addressable SLC with built-in isolators providing equivalent to Class A
classes.notes: Some codes accept Class C as equivalent to Class A

design_software_loading_check.description: Validation rules the design software must perform

checks.description: Total device current on NAC must not exceed panel NAC rating
checks.error_if_exceeded: NAC overloaded — add circuit or reduce devices

checks.description: Voltage at farthest device must be ≥ minimum operating voltage
checks.error_if_exceeded: Voltage drop too high — increase wire gauge or add circuit

checks.description: Total devices on SLC must not exceed loop maximum
checks.error_if_exceeded: SLC overloaded — add loop or redistribute devices

checks.description: Devices per conventional zone must not exceed 20
checks.error_if_exceeded: Too many devices on zone — add zone

checks.description: Battery must support total system load for standby + alarm period
